purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Catabolite repression (glucose effect) is a global regulatory mechanism that ensures preferential utilization of glucose over other carbon sources. The system operates through cAMP-CRP (CAP) complex that activates transcription of genes for alternative sugar utilization only when glucose is absent. This process integrates metabolic state with gene expression, affecting over 100 genes and demonstrating hierarchical carbon source preference.

name: Catabolite Repression and Global Carbon Control
notes: This flowchart represents the catabolite repression system with 33 unique nodes (A-AZ). Contains 2 OR logic gates (glucose presence check, cAMP level assessment) and 2 AND gates (lac and ara operon activation each require both CRP-cAMP AND specific inducer). The system demonstrates global metabolic control affecting over 100 genes, hierarchical carbon source preference (glucose > other sugars), and integration of transport (PTS) with transcription control. The mechanism explains classic diauxic growth observed by Monod. All mechanisms validated against genetic and biochemical studies.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on decades of research on catabolite repression, beginning with Jacques Monod's diauxic growth studies. The cAMP-CRP regulatory mechanism is one of the most thoroughly characterized global regulatory systems in bacteria. All components and regulatory mechanisms have been validated through genetic, biochemical, and structural studies.
